user_interventions_note: PLAN checkpoint default-skipped — no production paths, no critical priority, only 2 shards
cost_drift_note: shard 2 used only 54k tokens (1m52s) — much faster than read-only audits because scope was bounded write. v2.4 calibrate write-shard agent backend separately from audit-shard.
v2_4_calibration_findings: §17 cost: write-shard agent:general-purpose ~$0.05 (faster, bounded scope) vs audit-shard ~$0.10 (file-reading-heavy). Split into two rows.
§4.2 routing: queen-direct backend with estimated_lines<30 + obvious complexity worked clean — confirms the rule 1 cutoff.
DAG depends_on resolution worked — shard 2 dispatched only after shard 1 was MERGED.
First write-shard colony validates the §2.6 converge path (files_allowed gate, diff truth, queen-rerun gates) end-to-end without integration worktree (small enough not to need it).
Pytest fixtures and parametrize handled cleanly by agent ant — no need for specialist for stats-test work.
